Finding: Object placeholders are always projected as standalone blocks.
Impact: Text before and after U+FFFC is split into separate paragraph blocks. Inline equations/images cannot retain inline placement even though the schema supports inline display.
Fix direction: Represent inline objects inside paragraph projection spans, or explicitly remove inline-object support from the schema and UI. Do not force every placeholder through InputBlock.
Verification: Create `A[inline equation]B`, reopen, export, and concurrently edit around both sides.
Architecture relation: Conflicts with the plan's requirement that inline and block equations use the same placeholder and are rendered according to metadata.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: A placeholder with no resolvable block record silently disappears.
Impact: Canonical text can contain a structural anchor that is invisible in the editor, search, and export. A later edit may unknowingly delete or relocate it.
Fix direction: Project an explicit corrupt/missing-object block and schedule a Loro repair; never silently drop the anchor.
Verification: Delete a block-map entry while leaving U+FFFC, then reopen.
Architecture relation: The plan requires malformed state to be repaired before further sharing.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Malformed or unresolved object anchors are silently skipped.
Impact: Blocks with missing cursor bytes, undecodable cursors, or failed resolution become unreachable without a visible error.
Fix direction: Return structured projection diagnostics, render a recovery placeholder, and schedule canonical repair/quarantine.
Verification: Corrupt each anchor field independently and reopen.
Architecture relation: Violates deterministic rendering from canonical state and repair expectations.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Multiple object blocks at the same resolved cursor overwrite each other in a BTreeMap.
Impact: Only the last block survives projection, causing invisible durable content.
Fix direction: Store a vector per position and define deterministic tie ordering, preferably by CRDT anchor ordering plus durable block ID.
Verification: Insert two concurrent objects at one cursor from separate replicas.
Architecture relation: Object ordering must be canonical and deterministic.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Projection fabricates paragraph and block IDs from the current block index when metadata is missing.
Impact: The fallback IDs shift when structure changes and can be used by later commands, causing commands to target the wrong paragraph/block.
Fix direction: Treat missing durable identity as malformed canonical state. Schedule a Loro repair that creates durable IDs before accepting commands.
Verification: Remove paragraph metadata, reopen, insert an object before it, and compare IDs.
Architecture relation: All durable IDs must be stored in Loro; projection-generated IDs must not become canonical.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Invalid image asset IDs are coerced to AssetId(0).
Impact: Different malformed references alias the same synthetic ID, hiding corruption and potentially displaying the wrong bytes.
Fix direction: Reject malformed IDs or project an explicit invalid-asset placeholder carrying the raw value.
Verification: Set `asset_id` to non-numeric text and reopen.
Architecture relation: Asset identity must be canonical and integrity-safe.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Only the first image-caption paragraph is projected.
Impact: Additional caption paragraphs remain in Loro but are invisible and can be lost when the caption is edited through the one-paragraph UI model.
Fix direction: Change the image projection type to hold a paragraph flow, not one InputParagraph.
Verification: Create a two-paragraph caption, reopen, edit, and compare Loro flow.
Architecture relation: Caption is an independent paragraph-bearing text flow.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: `trim_start_matches('\n')` removes all leading newlines, not only the sentinel.
Impact: Legitimate leading blank lines in equation, alt-text, header, footer, or other flows are collapsed.
Fix direction: Remove exactly one sentinel code point when the flow kind requires it.
Verification: Create a flow with sentinel plus two leading blank lines and round-trip it.
Architecture relation: Projection must deterministically preserve canonical flow contents.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Images and equations inside table-cell flows are discarded.
Impact: Canonical nested objects can exist but vanish from the table projection, search/export view, and editor.
Fix direction: Extend TableCellBlock to represent all supported object kinds or prevent their insertion canonically with explicit validation.
Verification: Insert an image and equation into a cell flow, reopen, and export.
Architecture relation: Cell flows are continuous flows and the object-placeholder strategy applies to supporting flows.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Ragged or overlapping table topology is accepted without a normalization/repair phase.
Impact: Concurrent row/column/cell edits can produce missing cells, duplicate coordinates, invalid spans, or partial rows that render inconsistently.
Fix direction: Add a deterministic table-grid validator and canonical repair transaction; project explicit conflict placeholders until repaired.
Verification: Fuzz concurrent row/column insertion, deletion, span edits, and cell removal.
Architecture relation: Tables must remain structured, deterministic CRDT objects.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Sections whose start paragraph cannot be resolved are sorted to the end instead of repaired or rejected.
Impact: Dangling section metadata can appear attached to unrelated content.
Fix direction: Validate all paragraph references and surface/schedule repair for dangling section anchors.
Verification: Delete a section's referenced paragraph concurrently and reopen.
Architecture relation: Section boundaries must be CRDT-stable positions.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Editor construction immediately rebuilds sections from heading styles.
Impact: Canonical Loro page sections loaded by the projector can be overwritten by a derived outline before first render.
Fix direction: Separate page sections from heading outline. Never call `rebuild_document_sections` over canonical projected sections.
Verification: Open a document with a non-heading section boundary and inspect the editor projection.
Architecture relation: Page sections and heading outline are distinct concepts in the architecture.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Every structural projection patch rebuilds the document from blocks.
Impact: The rebuild discards section/page metadata and resets unrelated projection state and caches.
Fix direction: Apply structural patches to a frontier-scoped projection structure that preserves sections, IDs, assets, and page metadata; use full canonical reproject only as fallback.
Verification: Apply a remote object insertion to a document with section page settings.
Architecture relation: Projection caches are derived but must faithfully represent the same canonical frontier.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Section page attributes are defined canonically but are not consistently preserved through editor mutation and export.
Impact: The data exists in Loro yet becomes functionally dead or is lost in projection rebuilds.
Fix direction: Add end-to-end projection, editor, DOCX, PDF, and package round-trip coverage for every section field.
Verification: Round-trip non-default page size, margins, columns, orientation, numbering, header, and footer.
Architecture relation: The plan requires sections and page structure to operate from Loro state.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Clearing a header or footer does not remove the old flow-ID attribute.
Impact: Setting `None` leaves stale canonical references active.
Fix direction: Delete the map key when the new optional value is None; decide retention policy for unreachable flows separately.
Verification: Set header/footer, clear them, and read attributes back.
Architecture relation: Canonical metadata must reflect current state exactly.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Section numeric attributes are not range-validated.
Impact: Negative/zero dimensions, margins, columns, and numbering starts can enter canonical state and break render/export.
Fix direction: Validate and normalize at the semantic command boundary; retain raw malformed values only for diagnostics.
Verification: Inject negative and extreme values through Loro import.
Architecture relation: Runtime owns schema validation.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Incremental paragraph projection ignores object placeholders while full projection splits around them.
Impact: Full rebuild and incremental update can produce different paragraph indexes and text around the same canonical frontier.
Fix direction: Use one shared flow parser for full and incremental projection, including inline/object spans.
Verification: Edit text adjacent to an object and compare incremental result with a forced full rebuild.
Architecture relation: Deterministic rendering from canonical state requires equivalent projection paths.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: `object_input_blocks_from_loro` sorts objects by BlockId rather than body anchor.
Impact: Any caller treating this as document order receives registry order, not canonical flow order.
Fix direction: Rename it to registry-order explicitly or return objects in body-anchor order with reachability filtering.
Verification: Trace all call sites and compare order with a document containing moved objects.
Architecture relation: Body flow is canonical for body-object ordering.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Projection drift hash ignores sections, IDs, frontier, assets, and page metadata.
Impact: The self-check can report equality while meaningful projected state differs.
Fix direction: Hash all user-visible and command-targeting projection fields, or use a canonical serialized diagnostic projection.
Verification: Modify only section/page/ID/asset state and compare hashes.
Architecture relation: Projection integrity checks should cover all derived state.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Object drift hashing formats entire blocks with Debug.
Impact: Large tables and nested structures cause avoidable allocation and unstable diagnostic hashing.
Fix direction: Implement explicit stable hashing for each block type.
Verification: Benchmark hash on large nested tables.
Architecture relation: Observability should not create large hidden work.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: A semantic command batch is committed command-by-command and can fail halfway.
Impact: Earlier commands are already canonical, persisted, and emitted before a later command returns an error. The caller sees failure, undo grouping may be missing, and peers can observe intermediate state.
Fix direction: Pre-resolve and validate the entire batch against one frontier, then apply it in one Loro transaction/change and one UndoManager group. Roll back or fork a staged doc on failure.
Verification: Submit a valid insert followed by an invalid style/ID command and inspect Loro, package, UI, and outbound updates.
Architecture relation: The plan requires grouped semantic commands to become grouped Loro changes.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Normal edits routinely generate multi-command batches.
Impact: The partial-commit defect is reachable through ordinary replacements, formatting, and paste rather than only malformed API calls.
Fix direction: Fix runtime atomicity; do not rely on command producers to emit one command.
Verification: Exercise replace-selection, paste, and style changes with an injected failure after the first command.
Architecture relation: Semantic undo units must be atomic.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Queued edit batches are flattened across frontiers with only a debug assertion.
Impact: Release builds can combine commands constructed from different projections into one request.
Fix direction: Reject mixed-frontier queues in all builds and rebase each queued command through canonical cursors or resnapshot before submitting.
Verification: Queue a second edit after a remote update before the first local flush completes.
Architecture relation: Projection coordinates are valid only at their originating frontier.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Actor coalescing can merge distinct submitters that happen to share a frontier.
Impact: Only one response/event stream may represent multiple requests, and coordinate dependencies between requests are not explicit.
Fix direction: Attach monotonically increasing edit-request IDs; preserve one response per request or prove a single serialized producer invariant.
Verification: Concurrent-submit two batches from save/export and live flush.
Architecture relation: One runtime owner is correct, but request semantics must remain explicit.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: `set_author_identity` commits canonical Loro state without persistence or LocalUpdate emission.
Impact: The frontier advances outside the package update chain and outside collaboration. Later edits may be stale, and durable user/replica metadata can disappear after reopen.
Fix direction: Route identity changes through the same local-change pipeline as document mutations: capture from frontier/VV, append update, emit LocalUpdate, refresh projection if needed.
Verification: Join a session, set identity, close before editing, reopen, and inspect users/replicas.
Architecture relation: All canonical Loro changes must be persisted and synchronized.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Author registration is launched separately from the first editor flush.
Impact: A first edit can race the metadata commit and be rejected as stale or be acknowledged against an unexpected frontier.
Fix direction: Complete runtime initialization and identity persistence before enabling editor capture.
Verification: Join and type immediately under artificial actor delay.
Architecture relation: The editor projection frontier must match the live runtime before command construction.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Persistence occurs after the Loro mutation is committed.
Impact: A disk/journal failure leaves canonical in-memory state changed while the method returns an error and may emit no projection/network events.
Fix direction: Stage the update and package write before acknowledging success, or track an explicit durable-dirty state and still return canonical events so the UI cannot diverge.
Verification: Inject append failure after a valid command.
Architecture relation: Canonical mutation and durable append need a defined failure contract.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: `append_update_segment` mutates the in-memory package before validation completes.
Impact: A validation error leaves the package object partially advanced.
Fix direction: Build a cloned/staged package, validate it, then replace `self` only after success.
Verification: Inject invalid frontier/checksum state and compare package before/after error.
Architecture relation: Crash-safe package coordination must be transactional.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: `replace_assets_from_document` mutates package assets before all fallible validation work.
Impact: An error leaves asset indexes and bytes partially changed in memory.
Fix direction: Stage and validate asset replacement before committing to `self`.
Verification: Inject malformed asset metadata or index failure.
Architecture relation: Package updates must be all-or-nothing.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Checkpointing performs many fallible canonical/package mutations without a transaction boundary.
Impact: Failure after revision insertion, cache rebuild, compaction, or path change can leave an internally inconsistent runtime and misleading save status.
Fix direction: Construct a staged checkpoint package and write atomically; only then swap runtime package state and mark saved.
Verification: Inject a failure at each step in sequence.
Architecture relation: Explicit save is a revision boundary and must be crash-safe.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: A failed append can be followed by a later append from advanced in-memory state.
Impact: The disk journal can receive a segment whose `from_frontier` is not present on disk, creating a replay gap.
Fix direction: Track last durable frontier separately and refuse further appends until the missing segment is durably written or a full atomic snapshot replaces the file.
Verification: Fail one append, allow a later edit to succeed, then reopen from disk.
Architecture relation: Update segments must form a complete replay chain.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Compaction clears update segments and prunes snapshots before all cache rebuild work succeeds.
Impact: A later error leaves the in-memory package partially compacted.
Fix direction: Perform compaction on a clone and commit the clone only after snapshot, caches, indexes, and validation all succeed.
Verification: Inject search/projection cache rebuild failures.
Architecture relation: Compaction must preserve named revisions and latest state atomically.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Generic table replacement clears and repopulates row/column/cell containers.
Impact: Normal table edits lose granular CRDT history and turn concurrent structural edits into broad conflicts.
Fix direction: Implement dedicated row/column/cell commands over durable IDs and mutate only affected containers.
Verification: Edit different cells/rows concurrently while another peer resizes or moves a column.
Architecture relation: Tables must be structurally CRDT-native and not rewritten wholesale.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Alt text, captions, equation source, and table-cell edits use whole-flow replacement paths.
Impact: Concurrent character-level edits can overwrite each other instead of merging naturally.
Fix direction: Expose cursor/range text commands for every nested flow and apply LoroText insert/delete/mark operations.
Verification: Concurrent-edit distinct positions in each nested flow.
Architecture relation: These fields are required to be independent CRDT text flows.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Table editor commands address rows, columns, and cells by numeric index.
Impact: Concurrent reorder/insert/delete can make a stale command target the wrong entity.
Fix direction: Carry durable RowId, ColumnId, CellId and a base frontier/cursor in every semantic command.
Verification: Reorder a column remotely while a local cell edit is queued.
Architecture relation: Rows/columns/cells require durable Loro-native identity.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: The low-level DeleteRange path can delete sentinel/newline/object characters without first pruning or repairing related metadata.
Impact: It can leave orphaned block maps, paragraph maps, sections, or an invalid sentinel.
Fix direction: Reject structural deletion through generic text commands; use semantic join/delete-object commands that update all metadata in one transaction.
Verification: Delete across the sentinel, newline, and U+FFFC through direct runtime API and editor-generated ranges.
Architecture relation: Structural characters are not ordinary user text.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: ReplaceParagraphSpan is derived from paragraph-only spans and may cross object boundaries represented separately in the body flow.
Impact: A replacement around object-adjacent paragraphs may remove or relocate structural placeholders unintentionally.
Fix direction: Build semantic ranges from canonical flow cursors including object affinity, not reconstructed paragraph snapshots.
Verification: Paste/replace selections spanning text-object-text.
Architecture relation: Canonical command positions should use Loro cursors wherever possible.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Incremental index maintenance only shifts paragraph and placeholder positions.
Impact: Style, search, table, section, asset, and durable-ID indexes can remain stale after patches that do not trigger a rebuild.
Fix direction: Define per-patch updates for every index or conservatively rebuild affected indexes.
Verification: Compare every index with a freshly rebuilt runtime index after random patch sequences.
Architecture relation: The plan requires explicit projection indexes with reliable invalidation.
Latest review note: Text-length patches now update search_unit_spans as well as paragraph/object positions. The runtime still ignores ParagraphRuns, ParagraphStyle, ReplaceObjectBlock, and AssetArrived for the affected auxiliary indexes, so style/table/asset/identity indexes can remain stale.

Finding: The CRDT actor command channel is unbounded.
Impact: A blocked runtime can accumulate unlimited edits, imports, assets, saves, and exports.
Fix direction: Use bounded channels with backpressure and prioritize shutdown/import/control messages.
Verification: Pause actor processing and flood commands.
Architecture relation: Dedicated runtime ownership should isolate work, not create unbounded queues.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Subscription summaries and local update byte vectors are unbounded between drains.
Impact: Canonical mutations outside ordinary command drains can grow buffers indefinitely.
Fix direction: Bound buffers, coalesce summaries by frontier/container, and log dropped/coalesced counts.
Verification: Generate metadata commits without calling the normal event drain.
Architecture relation: Permanent subscriptions need bounded processing.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Undo/redo forwarding uses non-blocking bounded sends that can drop requests.
Impact: Rapid user input can silently lose undo or redo actions.
Fix direction: Disable UI while one undo is in flight or enqueue with an explicit acknowledgement/error.
Verification: Hold the actor and press undo repeatedly.
Architecture relation: Undo is user-visible state and must not be silently dropped.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: The runtime owns LoroDoc, UndoManager, permanent subscriptions, package state, and projection.
Impact: This is the correct core ownership direction.
Fix direction: Keep this boundary while fixing transactionality and event routing.
Verification: No defect; retain architecture.
Architecture relation: Matches the dedicated CRDT runtime requirement.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Selection affinity and visual gravity are explicit stored fields.
Impact: Caret intent can survive collaboration and undo instead of being guessed from direction.
Fix direction: Add full bidi behavior and more boundary tests.
Verification: No defect; test concurrent same-position insertion and wrapping.
Architecture relation: Matches the selection-affinity requirement.
Latest review note: Presence and undo now construct stable boundary cursors from the preceding/following text element; remote cursor resolution uses Event→Unicode conversion and preserves visual gravity. Two focused tests were added.

Finding: RTL/bidi gravity is explicitly simplified to neutral behavior.
Impact: Caret restoration and collaborative selection can be visually wrong in bidirectional text.
Fix direction: Integrate bidi-resolved visual positions into affinity/gravity mapping.
Verification: Add Arabic/Hebrew mixed-line collaboration and undo tests.
Architecture relation: The target selection model calls out bidi behavior.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Native save, export, and undo hooks discard RuntimeEvents returned by command application.
Impact: LocalUpdate, repair patches, selection restoration, and other events can vanish depending on which UI action flushed pending edits.
Fix direction: Centralize all runtime calls through one event dispatcher that always applies projection events, publishes LocalUpdate, and updates session state.
Verification: Type without normal flush, then immediately save/export/undo in a live session.
Architecture relation: There must be one local mutation/event path.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Checkpoint LocalUpdate events are not directly forwarded by save hooks.
Impact: Peers learn about revision/checkpoint metadata only through later digest recovery, and may miss it if the session closes.
Fix direction: Publish every LocalUpdate returned by checkpoint before reporting save success.
Verification: Save and immediately close the host while a peer remains connected.
Architecture relation: Canonical revision metadata must synchronize like other Loro updates.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Any local runtime error during flush detaches the whole collaboration session.
Impact: A disk error, malformed command, or partial batch failure destroys the active session and can strand optimistic UI state.
Fix direction: Classify errors: stale -> rebase; persistence -> remain connected and show durable-dirty; bad local command -> rollback/reproject; protocol lineage -> isolate peer.
Verification: Inject each error class during normal typing.
Architecture relation: Local persistence and network liveness must not share one fatal path.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Local package-write failures are treated as collaboration-fatal.
Impact: Networking is lost even though peers and in-memory CRDT may still be healthy.
Fix direction: Track unsaved state and retry persistence independently from the session.
Verification: Make the package path read-only during a session.
Architecture relation: Runtime should isolate CRDT work from filesystem failures.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Detach initiates an asynchronous final flush while removing runtime/session hooks immediately.
Impact: Final optimistic edits can complete after the session is gone and never be published or applied.
Fix direction: Make detach await an acknowledged flush or explicitly cancel and canonical-reproject before teardown.
Verification: Type and instantly leave/close under delayed runtime execution.
Architecture relation: No user edit should be silently abandoned during lifecycle transition.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Non-stale runtime errors complete the in-flight marker without restoring the editor from canonical projection.
Impact: Optimistic UI may continue showing content that was never accepted canonically.
Fix direction: On any rejected batch, replace/rebase from runtime projection and restore transformed selection.
Verification: Inject invalid command after optimistic mutation.
Architecture relation: UI projection is derived and must converge to runtime state.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: The acknowledge-without-replay path assumes local optimistic projection exactly equals canonical runtime output.
Impact: Canonical repair, normalization, or metadata-driven visible changes in the same batch can be skipped while the frontier advances.
Fix direction: Have the runtime label events as pure echo versus corrective projection; acknowledge only pure echoes.
Verification: Trigger a command that also repairs malformed paragraph/object metadata.
Architecture relation: Derived projection must reflect every canonical repair.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Acknowledge-without-replay returns early and ignores all other event kinds.
Impact: SelectionRestored, projection repairs, revision events, or other future event types in the batch are lost.
Fix direction: Filter only the local echo patch; process the remainder through the common dispatcher.
Verification: Return a batch containing LocalUpdate plus SelectionRestored/ProjectionUpdated.
Architecture relation: Runtime event handling must be complete and centralized.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Runtime recovery snapshots can run without first flushing pending optimistic semantic edits.
Impact: The recovery file can omit the newest unsaved edits, then record that generation as recovered.
Fix direction: Recovery hook must atomically submit pending edits, await canonical acknowledgement, and snapshot that exact frontier.
Verification: Type, block the normal runtime flush, wait for recovery, then crash and restore.
Architecture relation: Recovery must serialize canonical Loro state at the user's latest accepted edit.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Receiving out-of-band asset bytes marks the document as edited.
Impact: A cache/availability event causes dirty indicators, autosave, recovery writes, and close prompts even when canonical metadata did not change.
Fix direction: Separate asset availability/cache generation from document edit generation.
Verification: Open a document with a missing image, receive bytes, and inspect dirty state.
Architecture relation: Asset bytes are outside Loro and availability is not a document-history edit.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Applying synchronized assets clears editor undo/redo stacks.
Impact: A non-document cache arrival destroys user history.
Fix direction: Do not clear canonical Loro undo for asset availability. Invalidate only affected image layout/cache.
Verification: Make edits, receive image bytes, then undo.
Architecture relation: Undo is based on Loro document changes, not asset-transfer completion.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Remote structural patches rebuild the whole visible document while local optimistic edits may be queued.
Impact: Queued local text/object state can be overwritten or selections reset.
Fix direction: Rebase queued semantic edits over the new canonical frontier, or serialize remote application against speculative overlays.
Verification: Delay local runtime acknowledgement and apply a remote object insertion.
Architecture relation: UI receives derived diffs but must preserve/rebase local intent.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Broad structural changes clear active image/table/equation editing state.
Impact: Unrelated remote structure changes interrupt local editing.
Fix direction: Transform selected object IDs through the patch and clear only if the selected object was deleted/replaced.
Verification: Edit a table cell while a remote user inserts an unrelated image.
Architecture relation: Selection should be durable-ID/cursor based.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Projection apply is deferred during drag, resize, selection, and IME, but queue limits/order need explicit guarantees.
Impact: A long interaction under remote activity may accumulate stale patches or apply them after local coordinates changed.
Fix direction: Use a bounded frontier-aware queue and collapse to one full snapshot when the queue is superseded.
Verification: Hold IME composition/drag while flooding remote updates.
Architecture relation: Projection batching must be frontier-scoped.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Disabling runtime capture resets the in-flight count without cancelling callbacks or changing an epoch.
Impact: A late callback can mutate an editor after route/session transition.
Fix direction: Attach a capture epoch/token to every request and ignore late completions from older epochs.
Verification: Disable capture while a delayed save/edit request is in flight.
Architecture relation: Runtime lifecycle needs explicit ownership epochs.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: The editor retains a separate local undo stack whenever command capture is temporarily disabled.
Impact: A lifecycle gap can create edits in the wrong undo system.
Fix direction: Once a Loro runtime is attached, disable projection-native document history permanently; use a speculative overlay only.
Verification: Edit during attach/detach and startup transitions.
Architecture relation: Undo must be Loro-native for all documents.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Autosave directly invokes editor save while live edit flushing is independently scheduled.
Impact: Even with actor serialization, pending commands and returned events can be consumed by the wrong path and dropped.
Fix direction: Make autosave request a checkpoint through the same collaboration-aware flush/event dispatcher.
Verification: Trigger autosave while a local batch and remote import are in flight.
Architecture relation: One canonical command/persistence/sync pipeline is required.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Save-on-close does not explicitly forward checkpoint events before session shutdown.
Impact: The file may save locally while peers never receive final revision/edit metadata.
Fix direction: Await common runtime event dispatch and network enqueue before closing the session.
Verification: Close a dirty collaborative document and inspect peer/frontier state.
Architecture relation: Save and sync are expressions of the same canonical history.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Successful save/export replaces the entire editor projection.
Impact: This can reset transient local UI state and force unnecessary full layout work even when the projection is only an acknowledged echo.
Fix direction: Acknowledge the frontier and apply only corrective diffs; reserve full replacement for actual divergence.
Verification: Export a large document while editing and inspect selection/collapse/layout state.
Architecture relation: Full rebuild is exceptional, not the ordinary path.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Remote updates received before runtime attachment are stored in an unbounded VecDeque.
Impact: A peer can exhaust memory before document attachment completes.
Fix direction: Bound by message count and total bytes; spill/reject and trigger one version-vector pull after attach.
Verification: Delay attach and flood unique updates.
Architecture relation: P2P input queues must be bounded.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Malformed digest/version-vector data propagates as a session-fatal error.
Impact: One bad peer packet can detach a healthy collaborative session.
Fix direction: Drop/quarantine the sender, record telemetry, and continue the session; only local invariant corruption should be fatal.
Verification: Send invalid VV bytes from one peer in a three-peer session.
Architecture relation: Import/anti-entropy must be resilient to peer input.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Any remote Loro import error is escalated to session failure.
Impact: A corrupt or incompatible update from one peer disconnects everyone locally.
Fix direction: Reject the update, isolate the sender, and request a verified snapshot/update from another peer.
Verification: Send malformed update bytes while another peer remains healthy.
Architecture relation: Remote updates should be accepted/rejected without destroying runtime liveness.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Lineage mismatch is represented as a global GapAction rather than a peer-scoped quarantine policy.
Impact: A wrong-session digest can become a session-wide failure in the app layer.
Fix direction: Treat mismatch as invalid input from that endpoint; never mutate or detach the local session.
Verification: Inject a digest with another SessionId.
Architecture relation: Document lineage validation is required, but peer faults should remain isolated.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Direct payload reads trust a peer-declared u64 length and allocate before semantic validation.
Impact: A remote endpoint can request an arbitrarily large allocation and terminate the process.
Fix direction: Enforce protocol-specific maximum lengths before allocation; stream large payloads to bounded storage and use timeouts.
Verification: Send a length prefix near u64::MAX without a body.
Architecture relation: Network input must be bounded before allocation.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: A request handler can wait indefinitely for the application/runtime response while holding a serving permit.
Impact: Slow or wedged local handling lets remote clients exhaust service capacity.
Fix direction: Add per-request deadlines and cancellation; release permits before long app work when possible.
Verification: Never answer a direct request and open four more.
Architecture relation: Runtime/network isolation requires bounded waits.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Only a small global semaphore protects all direct serving.
Impact: A few slow requests in one session can starve all other sessions and message types.
Fix direction: Use per-peer/per-session quotas plus a larger global safety cap.
Verification: Open slow requests from one peer and test another session.
Architecture relation: P2P resilience should avoid cross-session head-of-line blocking.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: A gossip publish error exits the entire swarm task.
Impact: Transient oversize/backpressure/network failures kill collaboration instead of degrading one message.
Fix direction: Classify publish errors, retry/backoff transient failures, drop only the bad message, and keep the swarm loop alive.
Verification: Force one publish failure and observe subsequent traffic.
Architecture relation: Periodic digest is fallback liveness; the transport loop must survive errors.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Presence uses inline gossip payloads with a small limit.
Impact: Long display names or encoded presence state can exceed the cap; combined with fatal publish handling this kills the swarm.
Fix direction: Bound/sanitize names and selection payloads, or use a separate capped presence encoding with graceful drop.
Verification: Set a very long display name and publish presence.
Architecture relation: Presence is ephemeral and must never endanger document synchronization.
Latest review note: Presence snapshots now publish only the local peer state instead of encode_all, and external-caret work is debounced. Individual presence payloads, display names, the inline gossip limit, and fatal swarm publish behavior remain unchanged.

Finding: Version-vector digest size is not clearly bounded against transport limits.
Impact: Long-lived/high-replica documents may produce oversized digest messages.
Fix direction: Define maximum digest size and a fallback direct digest/snapshot negotiation.
Verification: Create thousands of replica entries and encode a digest.
Architecture relation: Anti-entropy must scale with durable history.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Network command and event channels are unbounded.
Impact: A blocked UI or slow network task can accumulate unlimited updates, presence events, blob requests, and status messages.
Fix direction: Use bounded channels, coalesce presence/digests, and apply backpressure to document-update producers.
Verification: Pause event consumption and flood updates.
Architecture relation: Networking should not create unbounded process memory.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: A single blob larger than the outbox byte cap is inserted and then immediately evicted.
Impact: Peers receive an announcement for content the sender can no longer serve.
Fix direction: Reject oversize blobs before announcing or store them in a persistent/streaming content-addressed store.
Verification: Publish an update just above the byte cap.
Architecture relation: Large updates/assets need reliable out-of-band availability.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Rapid blob churn can evict content before slow peers request it.
Impact: Peers stall until periodic anti-entropy reconstructs another route.
Fix direction: Retain until acknowledgement/expiry, pin latest document updates, or serve from package journal/content store.
Verification: Throttle a peer and publish many blobs.
Architecture relation: Asset/update availability is part of sync status.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: One global `pull_in_flight` flag blocks pulls from all peers.
Impact: A hung pull prevents recovery from healthier peers indefinitely.
Fix direction: Track pulls by peer/range, add deadlines, and allow fallback to another candidate.
Verification: Start a pull from an unresponsive peer, then advertise missing ops from another.
Architecture relation: Immediate anti-entropy must remain live under peer failure.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Lag recovery uses one fallback peer and has no immediate alternate selection in this state object.
Impact: Recovery waits on a poor candidate even when other peers are available.
Fix direction: Rank multiple candidates and rotate on timeout/failure.
Verification: Lag with two peers, make the selected fallback unavailable.
Architecture relation: P2P recovery should use available redundancy.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Presence-state keys are accepted from encoded ephemeral updates without binding them to the authenticated sender endpoint.
Impact: A session member may spoof another participant's name/caret/color key.
Fix direction: Wrap presence updates in sender-authenticated envelopes and reject states for keys not derived from the transport sender.
Verification: Have peer B publish an encoded state under peer A's key.
Architecture relation: Presence is ephemeral, but peer identity must remain trustworthy.
Latest review note: Publishing encode_self prevents an honest peer from rebroadcasting all remote roster states. Inbound ephemeral state keys are still not cryptographically/transport-bound to the sending endpoint.

Finding: Session tickets contain no expiry, capability, role, or revocation data.
Impact: A copied bearer ticket may remain usable for the session lifetime with editor-level access determined elsewhere.
Fix direction: Define signed capabilities/roles, optional expiry, and host revocation/rotation.
Verification: Reuse an old ticket after role change or host restart.
Architecture relation: The architecture defines roles/users but needs an authorization trust model.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: SessionId parser accepts uppercase hex while the error says lowercase is required.
Impact: Diagnostics and canonical ticket expectations disagree.
Fix direction: Either reject uppercase or change the message.
Verification: Parse uppercase A-F.
Architecture relation: Low-risk protocol consistency issue.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Received assets are checked against expected byte length and BLAKE3 hash.
Impact: This prevents accidental/corrupt asset attachment.
Fix direction: Retain and add size caps before allocation/download.
Verification: No defect; adversarially test mismatch handling.
Architecture relation: Matches canonical BLAKE3 asset addressing.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Pending/missing import dependencies trigger immediate anti-entropy work.
Impact: The design does not rely only on periodic digest.
Fix direction: Keep this path while making failures peer-scoped and time-bounded.
Verification: No defect; retain behavior.
Architecture relation: Matches the anti-entropy requirement.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: When block IDs form a single move, the diff emits only MoveBlock.
Impact: Text/style/object changes in the same canonical commit are omitted.
Fix direction: After structural detection, also diff all retained blocks, or derive patches directly from the Loro event batch.
Verification: Move a block and edit its content in one grouped change.
Architecture relation: A projection event must represent the complete frontier transition.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Insert/delete structural diffs omit changes to retained prefix and suffix blocks.
Impact: A commit that inserts a block and edits existing content produces an incomplete UI update.
Fix direction: Diff unchanged-ID regions before/after the structural middle or fall back to a full snapshot.
Verification: Insert an object and edit a paragraph in one transaction.
Architecture relation: Batched frontier invalidation must be complete.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Single-block-move detection is cubic and repeatedly clones vectors.
Impact: Large documents can freeze on common reorder diffs.
Fix direction: Use an O(n) position map/permutation check.
Verification: Benchmark one move in 10k and 100k blocks.
Architecture relation: Incremental projection is intended to improve responsiveness.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Object patch comparison pairs objects after sorting by ID, not canonical body order/reachability.
Impact: Registry/projection mismatches force fallback, and unreachable objects contaminate comparison.
Fix direction: Build object patches from reachable placeholder anchors and durable block IDs in body order.
Verification: Move/delete objects while leaving historical registry entries.
Architecture relation: Body flow is canonical for object order.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: ReplaceObjectBlock causes a full document structural rebuild.
Impact: Small caption/source/cell changes invalidate all paragraphs and transient state.
Fix direction: Patch object fields and nested-flow projections directly by durable block ID.
Verification: Edit one equation character in a large document.
Architecture relation: Full rebuild should be exceptional.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Fallback counters exist, but several ordinary structural paths inherently fall back.
Impact: Telemetry may reveal the problem but does not prevent frequent full rebuilds.
Fix direction: Set service-level thresholds and treat repeated fallback during normal operations as an error in tests/telemetry.
Verification: Measure typing, image, equation, and table scenarios.
Architecture relation: Matches the plan's observability intent but not its performance target.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: ParagraphRuns patches rebuild document sections.
Impact: Inline style-only changes trigger unrelated outline work.
Fix direction: Do not rebuild outline/sections unless paragraph semantic style or structure changed.
Verification: Apply underline/highlight repeatedly in a large outline.
Architecture relation: Projection invalidation should be narrowly scoped.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Incremental body parsing and full projection have different object behavior.
Impact: A forced fallback can visibly rearrange paragraphs around objects after apparently successful incremental edits.
Fix direction: Use one parser/data model for both paths.
Verification: Compare hashes before and after forced full rebuild at the same frontier.
Architecture relation: All projections of one frontier must be identical.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Invalidation ranges use Unicode positions while UI deltas use UTF-8 byte lengths.
Impact: The split is intentional in places but easy to misuse across APIs and can break non-ASCII caret mapping.
Fix direction: Encode units in type names/newtypes and forbid raw usize crossing boundaries.
Verification: Property-test random Unicode edits and cursor transforms.
Architecture relation: Projection-local coordinate systems must be explicit.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Remote text diff reduces the entire change to one common-prefix/common-suffix replacement.
Impact: Multi-range edits in one commit produce coarse caret remapping and excessive invalidation.
Fix direction: Use Loro text deltas/event ranges directly and preserve multiple edits.
Verification: Apply two distant edits in one remote transaction.
Architecture relation: Batched event processing should retain precise changed ranges.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: One structural patch invalidates all paragraph layout.
Impact: Object insertion/deletion/move scales with full-document layout and cache rebuilding.
Fix direction: Invalidate only affected block rows, neighboring paragraph layout, and pagination ranges.
Verification: Insert an image in a 50k-paragraph document.
Architecture relation: Incremental projection should preserve responsiveness.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Package decoding trusts file-provided chunk counts and lengths enough to drive large allocations.
Impact: A malformed or hostile .db8 can exhaust memory before semantic validation.
Fix direction: Set global file/chunk/count/asset limits and stream/seek chunks instead of allocating all payloads.
Verification: Fuzz count/length fields near usize/u64 limits.
Architecture relation: The custom container needs defensive partial reads.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Postcard decoding has no explicit nesting/string/container resource limits.
Impact: Hostile metadata can cause excessive allocation or CPU even when outer chunks are bounded.
Fix direction: Use bounded decoding wrappers or validate encoded lengths before deserialization.
Verification: Coverage-guided fuzz package manifests and indexes.
Architecture relation: External document files are untrusted input.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Integrity-index validation may not enforce one-to-one uniqueness between index entries and durable chunks.
Impact: Duplicate entries could satisfy count/membership checks while another chunk is omitted.
Fix direction: Validate uniqueness by `(kind,id)` and exact set equality.
Verification: Construct duplicate index entries with one missing chunk.
Architecture relation: Integrity index must cover every durable chunk exactly once.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: An empty integrity index is accepted as an older package.
Impact: This preserves an undocumented compatibility path despite the architecture's explicit no-old-format stance.
Fix direction: Either document this as v1 forward evolution or require the index for package_format_version 1.
Verification: Open a package with the index removed.
Architecture relation: The plan rejects old development .db8 compatibility; internal v1 tolerance should be explicit.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Reading cached search units first reads the entire package into memory.
Impact: Unopened-file indexing still loads all embedded assets and history, defeating the partial-read goal.
Fix direction: Read header/chunk table/manifest/search chunks with Seek without materializing unrelated chunks.
Verification: Index a package containing multi-gigabyte assets.
Architecture relation: The package was intended to support fast unopened-file indexing.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Normal package read loads the complete file and all asset bytes.
Impact: Large documents can temporarily duplicate the whole file, decoded package, snapshots, and projection in memory.
Fix direction: Implement a seekable package reader and lazy/content-addressed asset loading.
Verification: Profile opening a package with large images/history.
Architecture relation: Chunked format should permit partial reads.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Asset and chunk byte lengths are not governed by a product-level maximum.
Impact: A valid-but-huge package can force unsafe memory pressure.
Fix direction: Define configurable limits and stream assets from disk.
Verification: Open packages with progressively larger assets.
Architecture relation: Asset bytes belong outside the CRDT log, but still need bounded storage.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Asset attachment validates bytes but the exact asset-ID/content-hash association needs one canonical enforcement point.
Impact: Duplicate IDs or metadata races could attach verified bytes to stale metadata.
Fix direction: Require `(asset_id, expected_hash, byte_length)` equality at runtime merge and package write.
Verification: Race metadata replacement with asset arrival.
Architecture relation: Asset references and integrity metadata must remain coherent.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Initial package creation stores both latest and named-revision snapshots even when states are nearly identical.
Impact: Every new document starts with duplicate snapshot payload overhead.
Fix direction: Deduplicate identical snapshot bytes/frontiers and let a revision reference the latest snapshot.
Verification: Compare initial package chunk hashes/frontiers.
Architecture relation: Named revisions need restorable points, not duplicate identical storage.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Explicit save rebuilds projection cache, search cache, snapshot, indexes, and revisions synchronously.
Impact: Large documents can stall the single CRDT runtime and block typing/sync.
Fix direction: Persist the update/revision atomically first, then build disposable caches on a snapshot in background and append them later.
Verification: Measure save latency on large text/table/image documents.
Architecture relation: Disposable caches should not block canonical editing.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Whole-file writes use AtomicFile.
Impact: Final replacement avoids direct truncation of the destination.
Fix direction: Retain; add fsync/directory durability policy if required.
Verification: No defect; crash-test.
Architecture relation: Matches crash-safe save intent.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Journal format has transaction and commit markers plus checksums.
Impact: Incomplete tail writes can be distinguished from committed records.
Fix direction: Keep while enforcing continuity and bounded parsing.
Verification: No defect; truncate-at-every-byte fuzzing.
Architecture relation: Matches append-first crash-safe storage.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Named revision frontiers receive retained snapshots during compaction.
Impact: Named points remain independently openable after unnamed update segments are compacted.
Fix direction: Retain and add failure-atomic staging.
Verification: No defect; verify every retained revision after repeated compaction.
Architecture relation: Matches named-revision retention requirements.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Background search indexing rewrites the source .db8 to rebuild its search cache.
Impact: The indexer can race an active editor save and overwrite newer user content with the stale package it loaded.
Fix direction: Make indexing strictly read-only. Rebuild external Tantivy units in memory; only the owning editor/save pipeline may append package caches.
Verification: Open/edit a document while forcing a tub reindex of the same path.
Architecture relation: External workspace indexing must be disposable and must not mutate canonical documents.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Fingerprint/stat data can be captured before the indexer rewrites the package.
Impact: The catalog immediately becomes stale and can trigger repeated reindex loops.
Fix direction: Do not rewrite source files; otherwise restat after a successful owned write.
Verification: Observe watcher events during cache rebuild.
Architecture relation: Search indexing should be read-only.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: File identity relies on size and modification time.
Impact: Same-size edits or coarse timestamp resolution can be missed.
Fix direction: Include a cheap content/package-manifest generation hash.
Verification: Perform same-size replacements within one timestamp tick.
Architecture relation: External indexes must detect canonical frontier changes.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: All body paragraphs are processed before all object units.
Impact: Every object inherits the heading path left after the final paragraph instead of the heading at its anchor.
Fix direction: Traverse canonical body order once and emit paragraph/object units as placeholders are encountered.
Verification: Place objects under different headings and inspect indexed paths.
Architecture relation: Search units must be section-aware and body-order aware.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Search indexes every object record in blocks_by_id, not only placeholders reachable from current flows.
Impact: Deleted/unreachable historical objects can appear in search results.
Fix direction: Build reachability from active flow placeholders and nested-table refs; ignore registry-only history.
Verification: Delete an object anchor but keep historical block map.
Architecture relation: Only current-frontier projected content should be searchable.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Object search order follows sorted map keys.
Impact: Search result order and unit IDs do not match document order.
Fix direction: Emit units during body-flow traversal.
Verification: Move two objects without changing IDs and reindex.
Architecture relation: Body flow is canonical for ordering.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: The nested table parent-cell parameter is unused.
Impact: Search references cannot uniquely describe the nesting path and may hydrate the wrong preview/target.
Fix direction: Store a full ancestor table/cell path or stable nested-table ID chain.
Verification: Index two nested tables with repeated cell IDs under different parents.
Architecture relation: Search references should use durable structural identities.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Search unit ID includes sequential index and current frontier.
Impact: Any edit can change IDs for otherwise unchanged units, causing churn in external indexes and UI references.
Fix direction: Derive IDs from durable paragraph/block/cell/flow identity plus unit kind; store frontier separately.
Verification: Edit one early paragraph and compare all unit IDs.
Architecture relation: Search references should use durable IDs/cursors.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Unknown unit kinds are coerced to Paragraph.
Impact: Schema corruption or new feature kinds silently change meaning.
Fix direction: Preserve unknown kind strings or reject with a rebuild/version error.
Verification: Inject an unknown unit_kind.
Architecture relation: Search cache schema should be explicit and versioned.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: SQLite catalog and Tantivy document updates are separate persistence systems without one visible generation commit.
Impact: A crash can leave catalog and full-text index referring to different document generations.
Fix direction: Use a generation marker and recovery/rebuild transaction protocol.
Verification: Kill the process between catalog and Tantivy commits.
Architecture relation: Both indexes are disposable but should fail coherently.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Empty caption, alt-text, equation, and cell flows produce no search unit.
Impact: Stable targets can disappear/reappear across small edits and cannot be navigated as empty structural units.
Fix direction: Decide whether structural units need durable empty records; at minimum keep stable identity metadata outside Tantivy body text.
Verification: Clear and retype an indexed caption.
Architecture relation: Search cache should map durable units even when body text is temporarily empty.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Search flow normalization removes all leading newlines.
Impact: Leading blank paragraphs and exact cursor/text correspondence are lost.
Fix direction: Strip exactly one sentinel and preserve remaining flow structure.
Verification: Index a flow beginning with multiple blank paragraphs.
Architecture relation: Search projections are derived but should correspond to canonical positions.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Sentinel-only flows may produce cursor endpoints at or around the sentinel rather than the first visible position.
Impact: Navigation to an empty unit can land at an unexpected boundary.
Fix direction: Define and test empty-flow cursor semantics.
Verification: Navigate to empty cell/caption/header units.
Architecture relation: Canonical cursors need consistent affinity.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Cached search units are rejected when their frontier does not match the latest frontier.
Impact: Stale package search caches are not treated as canonical.
Fix direction: Retain while implementing partial reads.
Verification: No defect; keep tests.
Architecture relation: Matches disposable frontier-scoped cache requirement.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Image captions are not emitted.
Impact: Caption content disappears from DOCX and PDF exports.
Fix direction: Export caption flow paragraphs with the image relationship and semantic paragraph styles.
Verification: Export an image with a multi-paragraph caption.
Architecture relation: Exports must be generated from complete snapshot projections.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Table row spans and column spans are ignored.
Impact: Merged cells flatten or duplicate incorrectly in exported documents.
Fix direction: Emit DOCX vMerge/gridSpan from canonical cell spans and validate topology.
Verification: Export nested merged tables.
Architecture relation: Tables are first-class structured content.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Column widths and header-row/table style semantics are not fully exported.
Impact: Layout and repeated-header behavior change outside Flowstate.
Fix direction: Map canonical widths/style fields to tblGrid, tcW, trPr, and table style properties.
Verification: Round-trip fixed/fraction/auto widths and header rows.
Architecture relation: DOCX is a first-class external format.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Equations are exported as styled text placeholders rather than OMML.
Impact: Mathematical semantics and rendering are lost.
Fix direction: Convert supported LaTeX/MathML source to OMML or embed a standards-compliant equation object with fallback.
Verification: Export and reopen equations in Word/LibreOffice.
Architecture relation: Equation source remains canonical; export must render a real equation representation.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Section page size, margins, orientation, columns, numbering, headers, and footers are not emitted from canonical section metadata.
Impact: Page structure is lost in DOCX and therefore PDF.
Fix direction: Project sections distinctly and write sectPr/header/footer relationships at the correct boundaries.
Verification: Round-trip every non-default section field.
Architecture relation: Sections/page structure are canonical Loro metadata.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Image alt text is not reliably written into drawing accessibility properties.
Impact: Screen readers may not receive the canonical alt text.
Fix direction: Write descr/title attributes on docPr and preserve plain-text fallback only for unsupported images.
Verification: Inspect exported XML and test with accessibility tooling.
Architecture relation: Alt text is a first-class CRDT flow.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Image crop and sizing modes are only partially represented.
Impact: Fit-width/intrinsic/fixed layout can change across export.
Fix direction: Map all canonical image attributes to DrawingML extents/crop/alignment.
Verification: Round-trip each sizing/alignment/crop mode.
Architecture relation: Object metadata should survive external export where supported.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Only common PNG/JPEG images are embedded; unsupported MIME types degrade to text.
Impact: Valid assets can disappear visually.
Fix direction: Transcode supported source formats deterministically or provide an explicit export error/report.
Verification: Export WebP/SVG/TIFF assets.
Architecture relation: Export should not silently omit visible content.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: PDF export inherits all DOCX export omissions.
Impact: Captions, equations, spans, section settings, and some images are missing or altered.
Fix direction: Fix snapshot-to-DOCX fidelity first or render PDF directly from the complete projection.
Verification: Compare Flowstate render, DOCX, and PDF fixtures.
Architecture relation: PDF export must originate from the same canonical snapshot.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Embedded source recovery trusts declared original length and decompresses zstd without a strict output cap.
Impact: A crafted PDF can trigger a decompression bomb or huge allocation.
Fix direction: Set hard compressed/uncompressed limits, stream decode with a bounded writer, and reject length mismatches before allocation.
Verification: Fuzz embedded payload headers and compression ratios.
Architecture relation: PDF is untrusted external input.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: PDF generation writes to the final output path without a complete temp-file/rename transaction.
Impact: Failure can leave a partial export that looks valid by filename.
Fix direction: Generate in a temp sibling and atomically rename after successful close/validation.
Verification: Kill conversion midway.
Architecture relation: External export should not corrupt an existing destination.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Temporary sibling names rely mainly on timestamps.
Impact: Concurrent exports can collide.
Fix direction: Use tempfile/UUID-backed exclusive creation.
Verification: Launch multiple exports in the same timestamp window.
Architecture relation: Low-risk filesystem correctness.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Embedded-file lookup appears focused on a flat Names array.
Impact: PDFs using nested name-tree Kids may not be recoverable.
Fix direction: Walk the full PDF name tree recursively with depth/count limits.
Verification: Create a standards-compliant nested EmbeddedFiles name tree.
Architecture relation: PDF source recovery should handle valid PDF structures.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Run-length mismatch silently stops style mapping and leaves the remainder default-styled.
Impact: Corrupt or inconsistent import projection loses formatting without an explicit error.
Fix direction: Validate run lengths against UTF-8 boundaries and fail or report a structured import warning.
Verification: Feed mismatched run lengths and multibyte text.
Architecture relation: Import heuristics may drop unsupported formatting, but corruption should be observable.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Import returns a supplied projection alongside canonical Loro instead of always reprojecting the finalized Loro state.
Impact: Normalization/metadata repair can make the initial UI differ from a later reopen.
Fix direction: Build the initial projection from the final imported Loro frontier, or assert exact equivalence.
Verification: Compare import projection to `document_from_loro` for random documents.
Architecture relation: Renderer should always consume a projection derived from canonical Loro.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Section/header/footer import into the new Loro section schema is not demonstrably complete.
Impact: DOCX page structure may be dropped even though the target schema supports it.
Fix direction: Trace every sectPr/header/footer field into SectionMap and independent flows; add round-trip fixtures.
Verification: Import multi-section DOCX with distinct headers, margins, columns, numbering.
Architecture relation: DOCX import is first-class and sections are in scope.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: OMML conversion supports only a subset and unsupported constructs need explicit reporting.
Impact: Complex equations may silently degrade.
Fix direction: Return structured import warnings and retain source/fallback representation.
Verification: Corpus-test advanced OMML constructs.
Architecture relation: Unknown formatting/content may be reported or dropped, but should not be silent.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: PDF source embedding uses the new Loro-native .db8 package bytes.
Impact: The old final-state serializer is not embedded alongside it.
Fix direction: Retain and harden size validation.
Verification: No defect.
Architecture relation: Matches the locked PDF source-embedding clarification.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: DOCX import constructs the Loro-native document path.
Impact: Imported documents no longer need a second persistent canonical model.
Fix direction: Retain; make the final projection canonical-derived.
Verification: No defect.
Architecture relation: Matches the Loro-native import pipeline.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Daily log files have no retention or total-size policy.
Impact: Long-running installations can grow the log directory indefinitely.
Fix direction: Keep a bounded number/age/total bytes and expose cleanup failures.
Verification: Simulate many days or large-volume tracing.
Architecture relation: Operational issue outside settings UI.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Non-blocking logging can drop messages under sustained load without a visible dropped-event metric.
Impact: The exact diagnostics needed for collaboration failures may be missing.
Fix direction: Expose queue saturation/drop counts and retain critical errors synchronously or in a bounded ring.
Verification: Flood tracing while blocking the writer.
Architecture relation: Observability should remain reliable under failure.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: A corrupt or unsupported existing .fl0 file opens as a blank document.
Impact: A later save can overwrite the original file; the source comments explicitly acknowledge the risk.
Fix direction: Return Result to the workspace, open a recovery/error tab, and never attach the original path to the blank fallback.
Verification: Corrupt an existing .fl0, open, edit, save.
Architecture relation: Whole-app correctness issue; separate from the Loro .db8 architecture.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: .fl0 save uses direct `fs::write`.
Impact: Crash/disk-full interruption can truncate the only copy.
Fix direction: Use AtomicFile/temp+fsync+rename and retain a recovery file.
Verification: Kill during save and inject short writes.
Architecture relation: Whole-app persistence issue.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: .fl0 reads and parses the entire JSON file without a size limit.
Impact: A huge file can exhaust memory.
Fix direction: Set a maximum file size and use bounded parsing where practical.
Verification: Open oversized JSON.
Architecture relation: External files are untrusted input.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Legacy recursive migration can overflow the stack on very deep nested boxes.
Impact: A crafted legacy file can crash the process.
Fix direction: Use an explicit stack and depth/node limits.
Verification: Generate deeply nested legacy JSON.
Architecture relation: External file parser hardening.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: .fl0 autosave failures are printed but not retained in user-visible document status.
Impact: Users can continue editing believing changes are safe.
Fix direction: Keep a persistent save-error state and block silent close/discard.
Verification: Make destination unwritable.
Architecture relation: Whole-app durability issue.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Recent-file previews fully decode .db8/PDF source packages.
Impact: Opening a workspace with many large recent files can cause startup I/O and memory spikes.
Fix direction: Read projection/search cache chunks lazily with concurrency limits.
