Verification: Profile 100 recent multi-gigabyte packages.
Architecture relation: Chunked package should enable partial preview reads.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: A missing .db8 path is treated as a new blank document.
Impact: Typos, deleted recent files, or transient mount failures can be masked as a new document at the same path.
Fix direction: Distinguish explicit New from Open; show missing-file state rather than silently creating.
Verification: Open a stale recent-file path.
Architecture relation: Whole-app file lifecycle issue.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Preview, open, and tub indexing each perform independent package reads/decodes.
Impact: Large packages are repeatedly loaded and validated, multiplying latency and memory pressure.
Fix direction: Share a seekable package reader/cache keyed by path+frontier generation.
Verification: Profile one file appearing in recent preview, tub, and active open.
Architecture relation: Derived caches should reduce, not multiply, package work.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: A generated application log file is included in the PR change set.
Impact: It adds noise, may contain environment/session data, and encourages future log commits.
Fix direction: Remove it and add the log pattern to .gitignore.
Verification: Inspect PR changed files.
Architecture relation: Repository hygiene issue.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: The reviewed PR head exposes no meaningful build/test status beyond CodeRabbit in the connector.
Impact: A 186-file refactor has no visible automated gate proving cross-platform compile, tests, or integration behavior.
Fix direction: Require Linux/Windows build, clippy, unit, malformed-file fuzz smoke, collaboration loopback, and crash-consistency jobs.
Verification: Check PR status after adding required workflows.
Architecture relation: Process risk; the architecture plan explicitly did not define a general suite, but release confidence still requires one.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Current visible tests do not cover partial command-batch failure, disk failure after commit, hostile direct lengths, indexer/save races, or concurrent table/object edits.
Impact: The highest-risk paths can regress silently.
Fix direction: Add deterministic fault-injection and multi-replica model tests before merge.
Verification: Run the proposed scenarios under randomized scheduling.
Architecture relation: Not a plan violation, but a release blocker for this implementation.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: A second low-level SemanticCommand API remains and uses absolute Unicode positions.
Impact: It duplicates the editor command surface and is easier to call without durable IDs/cursors or a base frontier.
Fix direction: Restrict it to internal tests/migrations or delete it in favor of one semantic command API.
Verification: Search all non-test call sites and remove/convert them.
Architecture relation: The old canonical operation language was meant to be retired.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: `body_object` records block kind strings such as `table` in fields named changed_blocks/changed_tables.
Impact: The invalidation cannot identify the exact durable object and forces broad downstream work.
Fix direction: Carry durable block/table IDs and affected flow/range.
Verification: Insert/edit two tables and inspect invalidation payloads.
Architecture relation: Projection indexes are intended to support exact invalidation.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: StaleProjectionError reports only frontier byte lengths.
Impact: Different frontiers often have the same encoded length, making logs misleading.
Fix direction: Include hashes/short IDs and causal relation, not raw frontier bytes.
Verification: Trigger two same-length divergent frontiers.
Architecture relation: Observability issue.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Runtime events can carry full document and package clones.
Impact: Revision/fallback operations can create large transient memory spikes in actor queues.
Fix direction: Use Arc/COW snapshots, bounded channels, and references to immutable package snapshots.
Verification: Open/fork revisions in a large asset-heavy document.
Architecture relation: Runtime isolation should avoid unnecessary copies.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: One continuous LoroText per body flow with sentinel newline and semantic marks is the right concurrency model.
Impact: Paragraph split and inline insertion can commute naturally.
Fix direction: Keep it; repair the projection and command layers around it.
Verification: Model-check split/insert concurrency cases.
Architecture relation: Strongly desirable plan decision.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Loro maps/lists hold durable schema identity while UI data remains projected.
Impact: This removes the old local/collaboration dual-write source of truth.
Fix direction: Keep and remove remaining projection-native mutation paths.
Verification: No defect.
Architecture relation: Strongly desirable plan decision.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: The custom package cleanly separates canonical snapshots/updates/assets from disposable caches.
Impact: The shape supports revisions, assets, search, and integrity in one artifact.
Fix direction: Keep the format, but implement seekable/lazy reads and transactional staging.
Verification: No defect.
Architecture relation: Desirable plan decision with implementation hardening needed.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Semantic style marks use non-expanding configuration.
Impact: Paragraph/style inheritance is controlled by commands rather than accidental sticky marks.
Fix direction: Keep and add exhaustive split/paste style tests.
Verification: No defect.
Architecture relation: Desirable plan decision.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Time travel uses named frontiers/snapshots rather than undo state.
Impact: Restore/fork can remain separate from per-replica undo.
Fix direction: Keep; make revision events uniformly persisted and synced.
Verification: No defect.
Architecture relation: Desirable plan decision.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Durable user display names and replica mappings are stored in the document.
Impact: Saved/shared files retain collaborator identity metadata, which may be more persistent than users expect.
Fix direction: Define a privacy/redaction policy, stable pseudonymous IDs, and export/fork behavior.
Verification: Inspect package after collaboration and after fork/export.
Architecture relation: The plan asks for durable authorship, but product/privacy policy is still needed.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: The UI mutates a full projection optimistically before the runtime accepts the command.
Impact: Without an explicit speculative overlay/rebase model, this recreates a temporary second mutable document model and causes many error/event races.
Fix direction: Choose one: runtime-first edits, or a formal speculative overlay containing pending intents over an immutable canonical projection.
Verification: Inject stale/failing commands and remote edits during local typing.
Architecture relation: The plan says the UI owns a projection, not a second authoritative mutable document.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: The current projected editor type system is block-oriented and cannot faithfully express inline object placeholders.
Impact: The plan's inline-equation capability is not implementable by the current InputBlock/Block model.
Fix direction: Either extend paragraph runs with inline object spans now or lock the product to block objects for this pass.
Verification: Attempt inline equation round-trip and caret movement.
Architecture relation: Plan decision is desirable only with a matching projection/editor representation.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Projected tables expose positional rows/cells without durable IDs.
Impact: The Loro-native table plan cannot be completed by adapters around the existing type; the editor model itself must change.
Fix direction: Add durable row/column/cell IDs to projection and all selection/commands.
Verification: Reorder under concurrent cell edit.
Architecture relation: The plan's table model is desirable, but requires deeper editor rearchitecture.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Every explicit save is both revision creation and synchronous compaction/cache regeneration.
Impact: The revision semantics are desirable, but doing all disposable work on the edit runtime is not.
Fix direction: Commit revision/update fast; compact and cache asynchronously from a snapshot.
Verification: Measure save latency and collaboration pause.
Architecture relation: Keep the product semantics, change execution strategy.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Embedding a search cache is useful, but letting external indexers rewrite it is unsafe.
Impact: Cache ownership is undefined and creates write races.
Fix direction: Only the document runtime/save pipeline writes package caches; external tub reads or rebuilds its own disposable index.
Verification: Concurrent editor/indexer test.
Architecture relation: Desirable cache design with incorrect ownership.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: The no-legacy-.db8 decision is technically clean but requires unmistakable format/version UX.
Impact: Users can receive an opaque unsupported/corrupt error with no recovery path if any pre-release files escaped.
Fix direction: Keep the no-importer policy if intentional, but show exact format magic/version diagnostics and preserve originals.
Verification: Open old/development .db8 samples.
Architecture relation: Product-risk note rather than an implementation defect.
Latest review note: The latest two commits do not modify the code establishing this finding.

Finding: Presence and undo cursor resolution still cannot distinguish both sides of standalone object placeholders.
Impact: offset_for_body_unicode converts every resolved canonical position into a paragraph-only DocumentOffset. Positions immediately before and after an image/equation/table placeholder can collapse to the same preceding paragraph-end offset, so remote carets and restored selections can render on the wrong side of an object.
Fix direction: Resolve cursors into a projection position type that can represent Text(DocumentOffset) and Object { block_id, affinity }, then teach ExternalCaret and undo restoration to render both forms.
Verification: Add paragraph-object-paragraph tests for Before, After, and Neutral cursors on both U+FFFC boundaries, including concurrent insertion and undo restoration.
Architecture relation: The canonical object anchor is U+FFFC, so selection projection must retain object-side affinity rather than collapsing all positions into paragraph text offsets.
Latest review note: The new cursor conversion makes this limitation explicit. It may predate these commits, but it was not recorded in the original 169 findings.
